title: Empirical: D0029's prop-persistence assumption is false for ChatGPT-class MCP consumers — ams_join succeeds, ams_send/ams_recv immediately fail with 'Authorization bearer required'
content: Operator verification 2026-05-08 against worker/src/mcp.ts at commit cefef3a60fee82267f8583c088993d9292f5f1d9 (live main). The connected ChatGPT MCP runtime called ams_join({ magic_link, stream_name: 'chatgpt', self_subscribe: false }) and got back { ok: true, conversation_id: 'conv_01KR2STR424357ANQKWS', stream_id: 'str_6876b1ad12d5462003a8ab3ad0', stream_name: 'chatgpt', peer: 'tincan-...' }. The very next ams_send call returned 'Authorization bearer required for ams_send'; ams_recv returned 'Authorization bearer required for ams_recv'. Live source confirms: tool_ams_join writes the synthesized account_id, account_namespace, account_is_transient, prebind_ns, prebind_alias, prebind_permissive, prebind_conversation_id, and prebind_record_json into this.props as D0029 prescribed. ams_send and ams_recv schemas register only { data } and { wait_ms } respectively — no magic_link argument, no binding token surface. wireWs and joined are also instance-volatile per file comments (wireWs is re-dialed on first ams_join, not persisted). The continuity D0029 assumed across 'subsequent ams_send / ams_recv calls in the session' does not hold for consumers whose MCP runtime opens a fresh transport session per tool call — which is exactly what D0019 already named as the turn-based MCP client behavior.

title: D0029's 'non-extension' rationale collapsed because it inherited an unverified assumption about transport-session continuity from D0023's predecessor design
content: D0029 explicitly declined to extend the magic-link-as-argument auth path to ams_send / ams_recv: 'Once ams_join populates this.props, subsequent ams_send / ams_recv calls in the session pass requireAccount() via the same mechanism. Extending the magic-link-argument path to other tools would multiply the auth surface without adding capability.' The assumption that 'subsequent calls in the session' inherit this.props is true only when 'session' means in-process MCP transport session — which is not what ChatGPT-class consumers offer. D0019 (Cross-Session Continuity via Account-Conversation Keying) already named the structural mismatch: 'Turn-based MCP clients open a fresh MCP transport session per turn or per process restart.' D0029 was authored without integrating D0019's already-canonized observation. The compound failure is: D0019 was correct about the consumer behavior; D0023 shipped Path C around URL-route synthesis that didn't apply to ChatGPT (because ChatGPT cannot configure the magic-link as the connector URL); D0029 corrected the URL surface for ChatGPT (magic_link as ams_join argument) but inherited D0023's assumption that subsequent calls were free; D0019's contradicting observation was not surfaced when D0029 was written. Lesson: canon decisions that name a consumer flow MUST search for prior canon describing that consumer's transport behavior before inheriting continuity assumptions. Specifically, any auth-path canon decision must consult D0019 before assuming this.props persistence.

title: Author D0030 — extend D0029's magic-link-as-credential auth path to ams_send and ams_recv; prescribe SessionDO self-rehydration; explicitly retire D0029's non-extension rationale with empirical evidence
content: D0030 (this PR) overturns D0029's non-extension stance. The architecturally consistent fix has three parts. (1) ams_send and ams_recv accept magic_link as an optional argument, mirroring D0029's reorder for tool_ams_join. When no bearer is present and magic_link validates, the wrapper synthesizes the same transient account per D0028's deterministic derivation (account_id = deriveAnonId(pepper, permissive_token)), locates the SessionDO at (account_id, conversation_id) per D0019, and proceeds. (2) The SessionDO supports self-rehydration: when an authenticated send/recv arrives at a SessionDO whose wireWs is closed or joined is false, the DO transparently re-dials the wire and re-binds the stream using D0028's deterministic stream_id. Idempotent at the wire level — same magic_link plus same stream_name yields the same wire-level subscriber identity. (3) ams_create_conversation continues to reject transient accounts (the existing this.props.account_is_transient gate is preserved). Door-2 (persistent bearer) remains required for any persistent-identity operation. No silent downgrade: when a bearer is presented but invalid, the call returns invalid_credential at the tool level rather than falling through to transient synthesis. Reversibility: additive at the auth surface (existing URL-route + bearer paths unchanged); the SessionDO self-rehydration semantics are a behavior change but are themselves bounded by D0019's lifecycle rules (idle timeout, buffer expiration, account deletion).

title: Patch canon/constraints/portal-bootstrap-content.md to instruct ChatGPT-class MCP consumers to call ams_join({ magic_link, stream_name }) before each participation burst — the no-code workaround that holds against the shipped wrapper
content: The bootstrap content constraint is the AI-readable surface ChatGPT and claude.ai web Connectors read on first arrival at the magic-link URL. Today's prescribed text describes ams_join as 'on the magic-link route, takes zero arguments because the conversation is pre-bound from the URL' — which only describes D0023's URL-route path, not D0029's tool-argument path that ChatGPT actually uses. The patch adds a new section explicitly naming the fresh-transport-session-per-turn behavior of ChatGPT-class consumers and prescribing the workaround until D0030 implementation lands: call ams_join({ magic_link, stream_name }) immediately before any ams_send / ams_recv burst. This works on the shipped wrapper because (a) D0028 makes ams_join idempotent on identity (same magic_link plus same stream_name yields the same account_id and stream_id), and (b) D0029's reorder lets the per-burst join succeed without a bearer. The patch also updates the ams_join description in 'How to Join' to mention both forms (URL-route and /mcp + magic_link argument). This is a no-code unblock that ships immediately on canon-doc merge.

title: worker/src/mcp.ts implementation of D0030 is deferred to the next session; this PR is canon + journal + bootstrap patch only
content: The implementation surface (extending tool_ams_send / tool_ams_recv schemas to accept magic_link, generalizing the synthesizeFromMagicLink helper, adding the SessionDO self-rehydration logic for wireWs / joined) lives inside the existing P0002 step-4 mcp.ts rewrite scope per memory. The McpAgent migration (D0024) and D0030's prescribed semantics cohabit naturally: McpAgent provides the SDK foundation, D0030 prescribes the auth/binding semantics that ride on it. Both should land in one execution session against the 1,401-line baseline (PR #34 added prompts/resources/magic-link-prebind to the original 1,003-line wrapper). Splitting canon from implementation here keeps the canon-tier-1 review surface separate from the code review surface, and lets the bootstrap-content patch ship as a no-code unblock for ChatGPT today. P0002 step-4 acceptance includes implementing D0030's prescriptions.

title: Challenge findings (oddkit_challenge mode=canon-tier-1): D0030 must explicitly enumerate alternatives, name disconfirmers, scope the retraction condition, and acknowledge auth-surface multiplication risk
content: The canon-tier-1 challenge surfaced eight prerequisite gaps: missing evidence citation, missing confidence signal, missing disconfirmer, missing alternatives, missing risks/costs, missing reversibility framing, missing comparison-target naming, missing principle-anchoring sample size, missing retraction condition. D0030's structure must address each. Specifically: (a) Evidence — cite this journal entry and the operator's empirical verification artifact. (b) Confidence — strong empirical (verified against live deployment AND source). (c) Disconfirmer — if McpAgent migration delivers cross-transport-session this.props persistence for transient accounts, the per-call magic_link argument becomes unnecessary. (d) Alternatives — enumerate (i) extend D0029 to send/recv (recommended), (ii) issue an opaque binding token from ams_join required on subsequent calls, (iii) require explicit re-join before each burst (the bootstrap-content workaround scoped to short-term only). (e) Risks — auth surface multiplication, consumer chattiness (carrying magic_link on every call), SessionDO state lifecycle as in D0019. (f) Reversibility — additive at the auth surface (existing flows unchanged); SessionDO self-rehydration is bounded by D0019's lifecycle rules. (g) Comparison — D0023 (URL-route Path C), D0024 (McpAgent migration), D0019 (keying), D0028 (deterministic identity), D0029 (the predecessor whose non-extension stance D0030 retires). (h) Sample — two consumer classes match the structural pattern (OAuth-only MCP consumers per OpenAI Apps SDK + claude.ai web Connectors per anthropics/claude-ai-mcp#112). (i) Retraction condition — if the MCP spec evolves to allow per-call custom bearers without OAuth, the magic_link-as-argument shape becomes a transitional measure to deprecate.

title: Next session: implement D0030 against worker/src/mcp.ts as part of P0002 step-4 mcp.ts rewrite
content: The implementation work is the P0002 step-4 mcp.ts rewrite scope per memory — sized for fresh context. Acceptance criteria: (1) tool_ams_send and tool_ams_recv schemas accept optional magic_link argument. (2) When called without bearer and with valid magic_link, both tools synthesize the transient account via the same synthesizeFromMagicLink helper D0029 introduced (now generalized; tool_ams_join already calls this). (3) SessionDO self-rehydrates wireWs and joined when needed; idempotent on identity. (4) Existing flows (URL-route Path C, bearer auth on /mcp) continue working unchanged. (5) ams_create_conversation continues to reject transient accounts. (6) No-silent-downgrade preserved: bearer-presented-and-invalid returns invalid_credential rather than falling through to transient synthesis. Tests: extend smoke harness with the ChatGPT-shape flow — ams_join → ams_send (with magic_link arg) → ams_recv (with magic_link arg) — across simulated fresh transport sessions. The McpAgent migration (D0024) is an independent decision; whether D0030 ships before, after, or alongside the McpAgent migration is the implementation session's call. If alongside, both land in one PR. If before, the handroll gets the D0030 surface and the McpAgent migration inherits it later.

title: PR #69 on klappy/agent-messaging-service is the live observability surface; each gauntlet step lands as a commit so the PR body reflects observable progress
content: Branch: claude/d0030-extend-magic-link-auth-qp7p, draft PR opened at https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service/pull/69. Branch baseline: cefef3a60fee82267f8583c088993d9292f5f1d9 (the same commit the operator's empirical verification used). Step 0 (orient) confirmed execution mode strong; Step 1 (challenge at canon-tier-1) returned this row's prerequisites. Steps 2-4 are the artifact authorings (this journal entry, D0030, bootstrap patch); Steps 5-7 are audit/validate/PR-final. The PR is draft until validate passes. Per operator instruction, every step is its own commit so PR-level state survives if the chat session UI dies.
